name: Sony WH-1000XM6 Wireless Noise Cancelling Headphones
verdict: The best over-ear headphones overall — new driver tuning, foldable design, and noise cancellation that rivals Bose.
description: The Sony WH-1000XM6 represent the latest evolution of the most popular premium headphone line. The HD Noise Canceling Processor QN3 uses 12 microphones for 7x faster ANC processing than the XM5. Co-created with mastering engineers, the sound signature is detailed and balanced across all frequencies. The foldable design (new for this generation) makes them travel-friendly, and 30+ hours of battery with ANC on gets through transcontinental flights. LDAC support streams high-res audio wirelessly. RTINGS' #1 noise-cancelling headphone for 2026.
features: HD NC Processor QN3 with 12 microphones
Adaptive Sound Control adjusts to environment
pros: Foldable design is a welcome upgrade
cons: Touch controls have a learning curve
Can feel warm during long sessions

name: Bose QuietComfort Ultra Headphones 2
verdict: The best noise cancellation money can buy — Bose's ANC is still the gold standard, now with Immersive Audio and wireless charging.
description: Bose wrote the book on noise cancellation, and the QC Ultra 2 is the latest chapter. ANC performance is the best available — it silences low-frequency airplane rumble, mid-range office chatter, and high-frequency fan whine more effectively than any competitor. Immersive Audio adds spatial depth to music, and CustomTune calibrates the sound to your ear shape on every wear. The Gen 2 adds auto power-on (put them on, they turn on) and wireless charging case compatibility. 30 hours of battery, premium comfort, and the best ANC make these the headphones for frequent travelers.
pros: Immersive Audio adds real spatial depth
Most comfortable headphones for long wear
Auto power-on is a nice Gen 2 touch
cons: $449 is the highest price on this list
Sound trails Sony for music purists
Plastic build feels less premium than price suggests

name: Sennheiser Momentum 4 Wireless Headphones
verdict: The best-sounding wireless headphones — Sennheiser's legendary audio tuning delivers the most natural, detailed sound in this roundup.
description: If sound quality is your top priority, the Sennheiser Momentum 4 wins. Sennheiser has been making audiophile equipment for 80 years, and that expertise shows. The 42mm drivers deliver the most natural, detailed sound of any wireless headphone — instruments have texture, vocals have presence, and the soundstage is wide and immersive. ANC is good (not Bose-level, but effective), and 60 hours of battery life is absurd — charge once every two weeks. The foldable design and premium materials make them travel-ready. At $300 (down from $350 launch), they're the value pick for audio enthusiasts.
features: Sennheiser Smart Control app with EQ
pros: Best sound quality in wireless headphones
$300 is great value for this sound
cons: Touch controls on earcup can be imprecise

name: Apple AirPods Max 2
verdict: The best headphones for Apple users — USB-C, adaptive audio, and the deepest integration with iPhone, iPad, and Mac.
description: The AirPods Max 2 finally address the original's biggest criticism: the Lightning port is gone, replaced by USB-C. Adaptive Audio intelligently blends ANC and transparency based on your environment — walk into a noisy cafe and it adjusts automatically. The H2 chip enables Personalized Spatial Audio with head tracking for immersive movie and music experiences. The anodized aluminum cups and stainless steel frame look and feel premium. At $549, they're expensive, but for users deep in the Apple ecosystem, the seamless switching between iPhone, iPad, Mac, and Apple TV is unmatched.
features: Personalized Spatial Audio with head tracking
Anodized aluminum and stainless steel build
pros: Spatial Audio with head tracking is immersive
cons: $549 is the most expensive on this list
Heavy at 384g — noticeable on long flights
No foldable design — bulky to carry

name: Anker Soundcore Q20i Wireless Headphones
verdict: The cheapest ANC headphones worth buying — hybrid noise cancellation, 40-hour battery, and Hi-Res Audio for $35.
description: The Soundcore Q20i are the headphones that make you question why anyone spends $400. At $35, you get hybrid active noise cancellation (not just passive), 40 hours of battery with ANC on, Hi-Res Audio certification, and a comfortable over-ear design. The bass is emphasized (great for pop and hip-hop, less ideal for classical), and the ANC won't match Sony or Bose, but it genuinely reduces background noise on commutes and in offices. Amazon's #1 best-seller with 10K+ bought monthly. For the price of a nice lunch, you get legitimate wireless ANC headphones.
pros: Hi-Res Audio support at this price is rare
cons: ANC is basic compared to premium brands
Microphone quality is poor for calls

name: Sony WH-CH720N Wireless Noise Cancelling Headphones
verdict: The best mid-range ANC headphones — Sony's noise cancellation and sound quality at under $100.
description: The WH-CH720N brings Sony's noise cancellation technology down to an accessible price. The same Integrated Processor V1 from previous flagship models handles ANC duties, and the sound quality carries Sony's signature warm, detailed tuning. At just 192g, they're the lightest headphones on this list — you barely feel them during long sessions. 35 hours of battery with ANC, multipoint Bluetooth for two devices, and Alexa/Google Assistant support round out a compelling mid-range package. At under $100 on sale, they're the sweet spot between budget and premium.
features: Sony Integrated Processor V1 for ANC
cons: Earcups are on-ear, not fully over-ear for some

name: JBL Tune 770NC Wireless Headphones
verdict: The best headphones under $100 — adaptive ANC, 70-hour battery, and JBL's fun sound signature.
description: JBL's Tune 770NC pack a staggering 70 hours of battery life into a sub-$100 package. Adaptive noise cancellation adjusts to your environment, and Smart Ambient mode lets you control how much outside sound you hear. The JBL sound signature is bass-forward and energetic — great for workout playlists and pop music. The foldable design and lightweight build make them travel-friendly. The JBL Headphones app offers a full EQ, ANC customization, and a 'find my headphones' feature. At $80, they compete with headphones twice their price on battery life alone.
features: JBL Headphones app with full EQ
pros: 70-hour battery is unmatched at any price
Adaptive ANC works well for the price
cons: Sound lacks detail compared to premium brands
ANC isn't as strong as Sony or Bose

name: Beats Studio Pro Wireless Headphones
verdict: The best headphones for both iPhone and Android — USB-C, lossless audio, and full features on both platforms.
description: Beats Studio Pro solve a common problem: most premium headphones favor either Apple or Android. The Studio Pro work equally well with both. iPhone users get Apple's spatial audio and seamless pairing. Android users get enhanced codec support and the Beats app. USB-C lossless audio (when wired) delivers studio-quality sound regardless of platform. ANC is effective, the sound is balanced with Beats' signature punch, and 40 hours of battery handles a work week. At $250 (frequently on sale for less), they're the best cross-platform premium headphones.
features: Full features on both iPhone AND Android
Personalized Spatial Audio (Apple) + enhanced Android codec
pros: True cross-platform — equal features on iOS and Android
cons: Sound can be bass-heavy for some genres
Beats brand perception as 'bass-only' is outdated but persists

name: Anker Soundcore Life Q30 Wireless Headphones
verdict: The best budget ANC headphones for sound quality — hybrid ANC, custom EQ, and 50-hour battery for under $60.
description: The Soundcore Life Q30 have been a budget ANC staple for years, and they remain one of the best values in headphones. Hybrid ANC with three modes (Transport, Indoor, Outdoor) adapts to your environment. The Soundcore app offers a full parametric EQ so you can tune the sound exactly to your preference — a feature missing from most budget headphones. 50 hours of battery in ANC mode, 60 hours without, means you charge them twice a month at most. Business Insider's 'Best Budget' pick. At $56, they're the headphones to beat under $100.
cons: Bass can be boomy without EQ adjustment

name: SteelSeries Arctis Nova Pro Wireless Gaming Headset
verdict: The best headset for gaming — dual wireless (2.4GHz + Bluetooth), hot-swappable batteries, and ANC that works for both gaming and music.
description: The Arctis Nova Pro Wireless is the headset gamers and remote workers dream about. Dual wireless lets you connect to your PC/console via low-latency 2.4GHz AND your phone via Bluetooth simultaneously — game audio and phone calls at the same time. The hot-swappable battery system means zero downtime: swap a dead battery for a charged one in seconds. ANC is effective for both gaming immersion and office noise reduction. The retractable ClearCast Gen 2 mic is certified Discord-compatible with AI noise reduction. Premium drivers deliver detailed spatial audio for competitive gaming.
pros: ANC works great for gaming AND office
Best microphone quality in a gaming headset
cons: $350 is expensive for a gaming headset
Base station required for full features
